heading: Enforcement of drinking water regulations
label: 300g–3 Enforcement of drinking water regulations

children.heading: Notice to State and public water system; issuance of administrative order; civil action
children.label: a Notice to State and public water system; issuance of administrative order; civil action
i
children.text: for which a variance under section 300g–4 or an exemption under is not in effect, does not comply with any applicable requirement, orsection 300g–5 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for which a variance under section 300g–4 or an exemption under is in effect, does not comply with any schedule or other requirement imposed pursuant thereto,section 300g–5 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: If, beyond the thirtieth day after the Administrator’s notification under subparagraph (A), the State has not commenced appropriate enforcement action, the Administrator shall issue an order under subsection (g) requiring the public water system to comply with such applicable requirement or the Administrator shall commence a civil action under subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: for which a variance under or an exemption under is not in effect, does not comply with any applicable requirement; orsection 300g–4 of this titlesection 300g–5 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for which a variance under or an exemption under is in effect, does not comply with any schedule or other requirement imposed pursuant to the variance or exemption;section 300g–4 of this titlesection 300g–5 of this title

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: B .—Notice
children.text: If the Administrator takes any action pursuant to this paragraph, the Administrator shall notify an appropriate local elected official, if any, with jurisdiction over the public water system of the action prior to the time that the action is taken.

children.heading: Judicial determinations in appropriate Federal district courts; civil penalties, separate violations
children.label: b Judicial determinations in appropriate Federal district courts; civil penalties, separate violations

children.text: authorized under paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a), or

if requested by (A) the chief executive officer of the State in which is located the public water system which is not in compliance with such regulation or requirement, or (B) the agency of such State which has jurisdiction over compliance by public water systems in the State with national primary drinking water regulations or State drinking water regulations.

children.heading: Notice to States, the Administrator, and persons served
children.label: c Notice to States, the Administrator, and persons served
i
children.text: comply with an applicable maximum contaminant level or treatment technique requirement of, or a testing procedure prescribed by, a national primary drinking water regulation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: perform monitoring required by .section 300j–4(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the existence of the variance or exemption; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any failure to comply with the requirements of any schedule prescribed pursuant to the variance or exemption.

children.label: C
children.text: Notice of the concentration level of any unregulated contaminant for which the Administrator has required public notice pursuant to paragraph (2)(F).

children.label: D
children.text: Notice that the public water system exceeded the lead action level under section 141.80(c) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a prescribed level of lead that the Administrator establishes for public education or notification in a successor regulation promulgated pursuant to ).section 300g–1 of this title

children.heading: Form, manner, and frequency of notice
children.label: 2 Form, manner, and frequency of notice
i
children.text: provide for different frequencies of notice based on the differences between violations that are intermittent or infrequent and violations that are continuous or frequent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: take into account the seriousness of any potential adverse health effects that may be involved.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to the form and content of notice given under and in a manner in accordance with subparagraph (C); and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to the form and content of notice given under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Contents
children.label: ii Contents
children.text: The alternative requirements shall provide the same type and amount of information as required pursuant to this subsection and regulations issued under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Relationship to section 300g–2
children.label: iii Relationship to section 300g–2
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed or applied to modify the requirements of .section 300g–2 of this title

children.heading: Notice of violations or exceedances with potential to have serious adverse effects on human health
children.label: C Notice of violations or exceedances with potential to have serious adverse effects on human health
i
children.text: be distributed as soon as practicable, but not later than 24 hours, after the public water system learns of the violation or exceedance;

children.label: I
children.text: the violation or exceedance;

children.label: II
children.text: the potential adverse effects on human health;

children.label: III
children.text: the steps that the public water system is taking to correct the violation or exceedance; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the necessity of seeking alternative water supplies until the violation or exceedance is corrected;

children.label: iii
children.text: be provided to the Administrator and the head of the State agency that has primary enforcement responsibility under , as applicable, as soon as practicable, but not later than 24 hours after the public water system learns of the violation or exceedance; andsection 300g–2 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: be provided to appropriate media, including broadcast media;

children.label: II
children.text: be prominently published in a newspaper of general circulation serving the area not later than 1 day after distribution of a notice pursuant to clause (i) or the date of publication of the next issue of the newspaper; or

children.label: III
children.text: be provided by posting or door-to-door notification.

children.heading: Notice by the Administrator
children.label: D Notice by the Administrator
children.text: If the State with primary enforcement responsibility or the owner or operator of a public water system has not issued a notice under subparagraph (C) for an exceedance of the lead action level under section 141.80(c) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a prescribed level of lead that the Administrator establishes for public education or notification in a successor regulation promulgated pursuant to ) that has the potential to have serious adverse effects on human health as a result of short-term exposure, not later than 24 hours after the Administrator is notified of the exceedance, the Administrator shall issue the required notice under that subparagraph.section 300g–1 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Regulations issued under subparagraph (A) shall specify notification procedures for violations other than the violations covered by subparagraph (C). The procedures shall specify that a public water system shall provide written notice to each person served by the system by notice (I) in the first bill (if any) prepared after the date of occurrence of the violation, (II) in an annual report issued not later than 1 year after the date of occurrence of the violation, or (III) by mail or direct delivery as soon as practicable, but not later than 1 year after the date of occurrence of the violation.

children.heading: Form and manner of notice
children.label: ii Form and manner of notice
children.text: The Administrator shall prescribe the form and manner of the notice to provide a clear and readily understandable explanation of the violation, any potential adverse health effects, and the steps that the system is taking to seek alternative water supplies, if any, until the violation is corrected.

children.heading: Unregulated contaminants
children.label: F Unregulated contaminants
children.text: The Administrator may require the owner or operator of a public water system to give notice to the persons served by the system of the concentration levels of an unregulated contaminant required to be monitored under .section 300j–4(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, each State that has primary enforcement responsibility under shall prepare, make readily available to the public, and submit to the Administrator an annual report on violations of national primary drinking water regulations by public water systems in the State, including violations with respect to (I) maximum contaminant levels, (II) treatment requirements, (III) variances and exemptions, and (IV) monitoring requirements determined to be significant by the Administrator after consultation with the States.January 1, 1998section 300g–2 of this title

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: ii Distribution
children.text: The State shall publish and distribute summaries of the report and indicate where the full report is available for review.

children.heading: Annual report by Administrator
children.label: B Annual report by Administrator
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Administrator shall prepare and make available to the public an annual report summarizing and evaluating reports submitted by States pursuant to subparagraph (A), notices submitted by public water systems serving Indian Tribes provided to the Administrator pursuant to subparagraph (C) or (E) of paragraph (2), and notices issued by the Administrator with respect to public water systems serving Indian Tribes under subparagraph (D) of that paragraph and making recommendations concerning the resources needed to improve compliance with this subchapter. The report shall include information about public water system compliance on Indian reservations and about enforcement activities undertaken and financial assistance provided by the Administrator on Indian reservations, and shall make specific recommendations concerning the resources needed to improve compliance with this subchapter on Indian reservations.July 1, 1998

children.heading: Consumer confidence reports by community water systems
children.label: 4 Consumer confidence reports by community water systems

children.heading: Reports to consumers
children.label: A Reports to consumers
children.text: The Administrator, in consultation with public water systems, environmental groups, public interest groups, risk communication experts, and the States, and other interested parties, shall issue regulations within 24 months after , to require each community water system to mail, or provide by electronic means, to each customer of the system at least once annually a report on the level of contaminants in the drinking water purveyed by that system (referred to in this paragraph as a “consumer confidence report”). Such regulations shall provide a brief and plainly worded definition of the terms “maximum contaminant level goal”, “maximum contaminant level”, “variances”, and “exemptions” and brief statements in plain language regarding the health concerns that resulted in regulation of each regulated contaminant. The regulations shall also include a brief and plainly worded explanation regarding contaminants that may reasonably be expected to be present in drinking water, including bottled water. The regulations shall also provide for an Environmental Protection Agency toll-free hotline that consumers can call for more information and explanation.August 6, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: Information on the source of the water purveyed.

children.label: ii
children.text: A brief and plainly worded definition of the terms “action level”, “maximum contaminant level goal”, “maximum contaminant level”, “variances”, and “exemptions” as provided in the regulations of the Administrator.

children.label: I
children.text: the maximum contaminant level goal;

children.label: II
children.text: the maximum contaminant level;

children.label: III
children.text: the level of the contaminant in the water system;

children.label: IV
children.text: the action level for the contaminant; and

children.label: V
children.text: for any contaminant for which there has been a violation of the maximum contaminant level during the year concerned, a brief statement in plain language regarding the health concerns that resulted in regulation of the contaminant, as provided by the Administrator in regulations under subparagraph (A).

children.label: iv
children.text: Information on compliance with national primary drinking water regulations, as required by the Administrator, including corrosion control efforts, and notice if the system is operating under a variance or exemption and the basis on which the variance or exemption was granted.

children.label: v
children.text: Information on the levels of unregulated contaminants for which monitoring is required under (including levels of cryptosporidium and radon where States determine they may be found).section 300j–4(a)(2) of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: A statement that the presence of contaminants in drinking water does not necessarily indicate that the drinking water poses a health risk and that more information about contaminants and potential health effects can be obtained by calling the Environmental Protection Agency hotline.

children.label: I
children.text: exceedances described in paragraph (1)(D) for which corrective action has been required by the Administrator or the State (in the case of a State exercising primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems) during the monitoring period covered by the consumer confidence report; and

children.label: II
children.text: violations that occurred during the monitoring period covered by the consumer confidence report.

children.label: i
children.text: inform, in the newspaper notice required by clause (iii) or by other means, its customers that the system will not be mailing the report as required by subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: make the consumer confidence report available upon request to the public; and

children.label: iii
children.text: publish the report referred to in subparagraph (A) annually in one or more local newspapers serving the area in which customers of the system are located.

children.label: i
children.text: prepare an annual consumer confidence report pursuant to subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide notice at least once per year to each of its customers by mail, by door-to-door delivery, by posting or by other means authorized by the regulations of the Administrator that the consumer confidence report is available upon request.

children.heading: Alternative form and content
children.label: E Alternative form and content
children.text: A State exercising primary enforcement responsibility may establish, by rule, after notice and public comment, alternative requirements with respect to the form and content of consumer confidence reports under this paragraph.

children.label: aa
children.text: the readability, clarity, and understandability of the information presented in consumer confidence reports; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the accuracy of information presented, and risk communication, in consumer confidence reports; and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to community water systems that serve 10,000 or more persons, to require each such community water system to provide, by mail, electronic means, or other methods described in clause (ii), a consumer confidence report to each customer of the system at least biannually.

children.heading: Electronic delivery
children.label: ii Electronic delivery
children.text: Any revision of regulations pursuant to clause (i) shall allow delivery of consumer confidence reports by methods consistent with methods described in the memorandum “Safe Drinking Water Act–Consumer Confidence Report Rule Delivery Options” issued by the Environmental Protection Agency on .January 3, 2013

children.heading: Exceedance of lead level at households
children.label: 5 Exceedance of lead level at households

children.heading: Strategic plan
children.label: A Strategic plan
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Administrator shall, in collaboration with owners and operators of public water systems and States, establish a strategic plan for how the Administrator, a State with primary enforcement responsibility, and owners and operators of public water systems shall provide targeted outreach, education, technical assistance, and risk communication to populations affected by the concentration of lead in a public water system, including dissemination of information described in subparagraph (C).December 16, 2016

children.heading: Forwarding of data by employee of the Agency
children.label: i Forwarding of data by employee of the Agency
children.text: If the Agency develops, or receives from a source other than a State or a public water system, data that meets the requirements of that indicates that the drinking water of a household served by a public water system contains a level of lead that exceeds the lead action level under section 141.80(c) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a prescribed level of lead that the Administrator establishes for public education or notification in a successor regulation promulgated pursuant to ) (referred to in this paragraph as an “affected household”), the Administrator shall require an appropriate employee of the Agency to forward the data, and information on the sampling techniques used to obtain the data, to the owner or operator of the public water system and the State in which the affected household is located within a time period determined by the Administrator.section 300g–1(b)(3)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 300g–1 of this title

children.heading: Dissemination of information by owner or operator
children.label: ii Dissemination of information by owner or operator
I
children.text: receives data and information under clause (i); and

children.label: aa
children.text: with respect to the concentration of lead in the drinking water of the affected households; and

children.label: bb
children.text: that the concentration of lead in the drinking water of the affected households exceeds the lead action level under section 141.80(c) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a prescribed level of lead that the Administrator establishes for public education or notification in a successor regulation promulgated pursuant to ).section 300g–1 of this title

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: I Deadline
children.text: If the owner or operator of the public water system does not disseminate to the affected households the information described in subparagraph (C) as required under clause (ii) within the time period established by the Administrator, not later than 24 hours after the Administrator becomes aware of the failure by the owner or operator of the public water system to disseminate the information, the Administrator shall consult, within a period not to exceed 24 hours, with the applicable Governor to develop a plan, in accordance with the strategic plan, to disseminate the information to the affected households not later than 24 hours after the end of the consultation period.

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: II Delegation
children.text: The Administrator may only delegate the duty to consult under subclause (I) to an employee of the Agency who, as of the date of the delegation, works in the Office of Water at the headquarters of the Agency.

children.label: I
children.text: the owner or operator of the public water system does not disseminate the information to the affected households within the time period determined by the Administrator, as required by clause (ii); and

children.label: aa
children.text: the Administrator and the applicable Governor do not agree on a plan described in clause (iii)(I) during the consultation period under that clause; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the applicable Governor does not disseminate the information within 24 hours after the end of the consultation period.

children.label: i
children.text: a clear explanation of the potential adverse effects on human health of drinking water that contains a concentration of lead that exceeds the lead action level under section 141.80(c) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a prescribed level of lead that the Administrator establishes for public education or notification in a successor regulation promulgated pursuant to );section 300g–1 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the steps that the owner or operator of the public water system is taking to mitigate the concentration of lead; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the necessity of seeking alternative water supplies until the date on which the concentration of lead is mitigated.

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: 6 Privacy
children.text: Any notice to the public or an affected household under this subsection shall protect the privacy of individual customer information.

children.heading: Notice of noncompliance with secondary drinking water regulations
children.label: d Notice of noncompliance with secondary drinking water regulations
children.text: Whenever, on the basis of information available to him, the Administrator finds that within a reasonable time after national secondary drinking water regulations have been promulgated, one or more public water systems in a State do not comply with such secondary regulations, and that such noncompliance appears to result from a failure of such State to take reasonable action to assure that public water systems throughout such State meet such secondary regulations, he shall so notify the State.

children.heading: State authority to adopt or enforce laws or regulations respecting drinking water regulations or public water systems unaffected
children.label: e State authority to adopt or enforce laws or regulations respecting drinking water regulations or public water systems unaffected
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall diminish any authority of a State or political subdivision to adopt or enforce any law or regulation respecting drinking water regulations or public water systems, but no such law or regulation shall relieve any person of any requirement otherwise applicable under this subchapter.

children.heading: Notice and public hearing; availability of recommendations transmitted to State and public water system
children.label: f Notice and public hearing; availability of recommendations transmitted to State and public water system

children.text: the ways in which such system can within the earliest feasible time be brought into compliance with the regulation or requirement with respect to which such finding was made, and

the means for the maximum feasible protection of the public health during any period in which such system is not in compliance with a national primary drinking water regulation or requirement applicable to a variance or exemption.

children.heading: Administrative order requiring compliance; notice and hearing; civil penalty; civil actions
children.label: g Administrative order requiring compliance; notice and hearing; civil penalty; civil actions

children.text: In any case in which the Administrator is authorized to bring a civil action under this section or under with respect to any applicable requirement, the Administrator also may issue an order to require compliance with such applicable requirement.section 300j–4 of this title

An order issued under this subsection shall not take effect, in the case of a State having primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems in that State, until after the Administrator has provided the State with an opportunity to confer with the Administrator regarding the order. A copy of any order issued under this subsection shall be sent to the appropriate State agency of the State involved if the State has primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems in that State. Any order issued under this subsection shall state with reasonable specificity the nature of the violation. In any case in which an order under this subsection is issued to a corporation, a copy of such order shall be issued to appropriate corporate officers.

children.label: A
children.text: Any person who violates, or fails or refuses to comply with, an order under this subsection shall be liable to the United States for a civil penalty of not more than $25,000 per day of violation.

children.label: B
children.text: In a case in which a civil penalty sought by the Administrator under this paragraph does not exceed $5,000, the penalty shall be assessed by the Administrator after notice and opportunity for a public hearing (unless the person against whom the penalty is assessed requests a hearing on the record in accordance with ). In a case in which a civil penalty sought by the Administrator under this paragraph exceeds $5,000, but does not exceed $25,000, the penalty shall be assessed by the Administrator after notice and opportunity for a hearing on the record in accordance with .section 554 of title 5section 554 of title 5

children.label: C
children.text: Whenever any civil penalty sought by the Administrator under this subsection for a violation of an applicable requirement exceeds $25,000, the penalty shall be assessed by a civil action brought by the Administrator in the appropriate United States district court (as determined under the provisions of title 28).

children.label: D
children.text: If any person fails to pay an assessment of a civil penalty after it has become a final and unappealable order, or after the appropriate court of appeals has entered final judgment in favor of the Administrator, the Attorney General shall recover the amount for which such person is liable in any appropriate district court of the United States. In any such action, the validity and appropriateness of the final order imposing the civil penalty shall not be subject to review.

children.label: A
children.text: the physical consolidation of the system with 1 or more other systems;

children.label: B
children.text: the consolidation of significant management and administrative functions of the system with 1 or more other systems;

children.label: C
children.text: the transfer of ownership of the system that may reasonably be expected to improve drinking water quality; or

children.label: D
children.text: entering into a contractual agreement for significant management or administrative functions of the system to correct violations identified in the plan.

children.heading: Consequences of approval
children.label: 2 Consequences of approval
children.text: If the State or the Administrator approves a plan pursuant to paragraph (1), no enforcement action shall be taken pursuant to this part with respect to a specific violation identified in the approved plan prior to the date that is the earlier of the date on which consolidation is completed according to the plan or the date that is 2 years after the plan is approved.

children.label: I
children.text: has repeatedly violated one or more national primary drinking water regulations and such repeated violations are likely to adversely affect human health; and

children.label: aa
children.text: is unable or unwilling to take feasible and affordable actions, as determined by the State with primary enforcement responsibility or the Administrator (if the State does not have primary enforcement responsibility), that will result in the public water system complying with the national primary drinking water regulations described in subclause (I), including accessing technical assistance and financial assistance through the State loan fund pursuant to ; orsection 300j–12 of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: has already undertaken actions described in item (aa) without achieving compliance;

children.label: ii
children.text: such consolidation, transfer, or other action is feasible; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such consolidation, transfer, or other action could result in greater compliance with national primary drinking water regulations.

children.heading: Tailoring of assessments
children.label: B Tailoring of assessments
children.text: Requirements for any assessment to be conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be tailored with respect to the size, type, and characteristics, of the public water system to be assessed.

children.heading: Approved entities
children.label: C Approved entities
children.text: An assessment conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A) may be conducted by an entity approved by the State requiring such assessment (or the Administrator, if the State does not have primary enforcement responsibility), which may include such State (or the Administrator, as applicable), the public water system, or a third party.

children.heading: Burden of assessments
children.label: D Burden of assessments
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that any assessment required pursuant to subparagraph (A) should not be overly burdensome on the public water system that is assessed.

children.heading: Financial assistance
children.label: 4 Financial assistance
children.text: Notwithstanding , a public water system undertaking consolidation or transfer of ownership or other actions pursuant to an assessment completed under paragraph (3) may receive a loan described in to carry out such consolidation, transfer, or other action.section 300j–12(a)(3) of this titlesection 300j–12(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: any potential and existing liability for penalties and damages arising from each specific violation identified in the plan of which the owner or operator is aware; and

children.label: II
children.text: any funds or other assets that are available to satisfy such liability, as of the date of submission of such plan, to the public water system that committed such violation.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: ii Inclusion
children.text: In carrying out clause (i), the owner or operator shall take reasonable steps to ensure that all potential and existing liabilities for penalties and damages arising from each specific violation identified in the plan are identified.

children.heading: Reservation of funds
children.label: B Reservation of funds
children.text: A public water system that, consistent with the findings of an assessment conducted pursuant to paragraph (3), has completed the actions under a plan submitted and approved pursuant to this subsection shall not be liable under this subchapter for a violation of this subchapter identified in the plan, except to the extent to which funds or other assets are identified pursuant to subparagraph (A)(i)(II) as available to satisfy such liability.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 6 Regulations
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Administrator shall promulgate regulations to implement paragraphs (3), (4), and (5).October 23, 2018
a requirement of section 300g–1, 300g–3, 300g–4, 300g–5, 300g–6, 300i–2, 300j, or 300j–4 of this title;
a regulation promulgated pursuant to a section referred to in paragraph (1);
a schedule or requirement imposed pursuant to a section referred to in paragraph (1); and

a requirement of, or permit issued under, an applicable State program for which the Administrator has made a determination that the requirements of have been satisfied, or an applicable State program approved pursuant to this part.section 300g–2 of this title

children.heading: Improved accuracy and availability of compliance monitoring data
children.label: j Improved accuracy and availability of compliance monitoring data
A
children.text: by public water systems to States; or

children.label: B
children.text: by States to the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of submitted data described in paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: by States and public water systems in implementing an electronic system for submitting such data, including the technical and economic feasibility of implementing such a system; and

children.label: C
children.text: by users of such electronic systems in being able to access such data.

children.label: A
children.text: a summary of the findings of the evaluation under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations on practicable, cost-effective methods and means that can be employed to improve the accuracy and availability of submitted data described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 4 Consultation
children.text: In developing the strategic plan under paragraph (1), the Administrator may, as appropriate, consult with States or other Federal agencies that have experience using practicable methods and means to improve the accuracy and availability of submitted data described in such paragraph.
